fresh_facts.claim: Iran seized or attacked at least two cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22, 2026, hours after US President Trump extended a ceasefire indefinitely.
The IRGC escorted two seized cargo ships to Iranian waters for inspections on April 22, 2026.
US forces maintain a blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz as of April 22, 2026.
Over 10,000 US troops were deploying toward the Middle East as of April 22, 2026.
Trump directed maintenance of the blockade of Iran following the ceasefire extension announcement on April 22, 2026.
Iranian officials dismissed the US ceasefire extension as a tactic to buy time for strikes on April 22, 2026.
IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Majid Mousavi claimed on April 22, 2026, that missile and drone launcher replenishment is faster than pre-war levels, reaching 70% pre-war strength.
The US-Israel strikes on February 28, 2026, killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and senior Iranian officials.
An Interim Leadership Council consisting of President Masoud Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Gholam-Hossei Mohseni-Eje'i, and Guardian Council representative Alireza Arafi currently manages Iran.
The Assembly of Experts is seeking a new Supreme Leader with no established timeline or identified frontrunner as of April 22, 2026.
Trump stated that Iran's government is 'seriously fractured' on April 22, 2026.
Trump claimed Iran is losing $500 million per day from US-imposed blockades as of April 22, 2026.
Trump extended the Iran ceasefire at Pakistani mediators' request but issued an ultimatum of 'another three to five days' for a deal.
Italy's Foreign Minister Tajani discussed ceasefire, nuclear assurances, and stability with Iran's Araghchi as of April 22, 2026.
China backed diplomatic efforts for a lasting US-Iran ceasefire as of April 22, 2026.
Pakistan prepared high-level security for possible US-Iran talks in Islamabad as of April 22, 2026.
Trump's negotiators reported fearing 'no one in Tehran to approve deal' due to fractured Iranian leadership as of April 22, 2026.
The US blocked Iraq's dollar shipments to squeeze Iran-backed factions as of April 22, 2026.
Iran's regime ordered 'shoot to kill' on protesters on January 9, 2026.
Senior Iranian officials have sought Chinese, Russian, and North Korean assistance with repression techniques as of February 3, 2026.
An estimated 800 to 5,000 Iraqi militias entered Iran to support regime repression efforts as of February 3, 2026.
Iranian security officials have been arrested for refusing to fire on protesters as of February 3, 2026.
Prediction markets showed regime fall odds at 3.2% by May 31, 2026, as of April 22, 2026.
Prediction markets showed Reza Pahlavi regime replacement odds at 4.5% by June 30, 2026, as of April 22, 2026.
Iraqi militias linked to Iran formed a 'joint operations' room as of April 23, 2026, at 09:24 UTC.
Germany halved its 2026 growth forecast due to fallout from US-Iran tensions as of April 22, 2026.
Brazil's exports fell 26% to $882 million due to fallout from US-Iran tensions as of April 22, 2026.
Iran's stability index recorded -1.58 in 2024, marked as the weakest on record as of April 22, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an acute multi-front crisis as the ceasefire expires with direct US-Iran military confrontation escalating, while Tehran simultaneously confronts internal instability marked by protest crackdowns, security force defections, and accelerating economic collapse from sanctions and Hormuz blockade. The regime's fractured leadership—with Khamenei assassinated in February and an interim council managing affairs—undermines both internal cohesion and negotiating authority while Iraqi militias form joint operations rooms in support.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 23, 2026, Iraqi militias linked to Iran formed a 'joint operations' room, coinciding with US maintenance of blockades and Trump's ultimatum of 'another three to five days' for a diplomatic deal, signaling imminent military escalation and regime coordination breakdown.
